description_long: This object is a slender, pointed implement resembling a primitive knife or stake, with a roughly cylindrical body that tapers to a sharp tip. The surface is mottled with a mix of earthy brown and pale gray tones, suggesting it is made from stone or bone, possibly weathered or aged. The blunt end appears irregular and slightly flattened, lacking any clear ergonomic handle or grip, and there is no visible guard or crossguard. The blade is straight and narrows gradually, with no serrations or decorative features. Its overall form is utilitarian, with a rough, matte finish and a visibly hard, unpolished texture. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for handheld use in cutting or piercing tasks.
description: A slender, pointed, stone-like knife or stake with a rough, mottled surface and no visible handle or ornamentation. The blade is straight and tapers to a sharp tip, with a blunt, irregular end.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, straight taper, and mottled coloration of the blade.
description_view_1: End-on view revealing the blunt, irregularly shaped base and cylindrical cross-section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again highlighting the straight, pointed blade and rough surface.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the narrow, elongated profile and gradual taper to the tip.
attribute_handle_design: irregular, blunt end with no distinct handle or grip features
